title: 3/22/19 Google Earthing Mountains of Boulder still

transcript.text: So, it's March 22nd, 2019, Friday, 2.43 p.m., and daily vlog living in Boulder.
I'm just about to go to my mother's house in a half hour because I have to do things for her, find things.
I'm kind of high off of my reefer gummies.
That's all I have left on reefer gummies, and then I'm out.
Then I have to quit.
So, I finished my mountains.
I went to mountain 400, and now I'm doing the filler in mountains, so it's like 50 or whatever.
But once I got to the inner part of the alphabet, I could find other mountains that I could copy and paste off of and modify slightly.
So, it was going by a little bit faster.
I wasn't having to trace everything from scratch.
So, yeah.
But, yeah.
I think I can finish these mountains and stay on track and finish all these 400 mountains and the roots out there and back and make the individual files of them.
And then the file of the – where it's just the – like of each mountain of the – broken up into three different colors, but then also each mountain for their own colors and all that stuff.
So, yeah, in just a few days, I'll be able – so I think I was on my one-week ATA, so I'm good.
And then after that, I'm going to fix it up on the page, on the web page, make it by – after organizing it alphabetically, then do it by height and then by running percentage, I guess.
And, yeah, so – and then I can move on to the other things and see how those are doing, you know.
I'm in the mood.

If I – if I – the good thing is if I stay on a high – on a fast pace and I get done with – I knock something out from limbo to pretty much done in two weeks or a week, then I'll – it'll motivate me to do the next one like that, you know.
And then maybe I can get the next one knocked out in two weeks to two weeks and then – and then I'll go back to Rome and I can do that too and all of them, you know.
So after, like, all said and done, just after another year or two, I should get my – my – definitely get my website to the next level for sure.
And then at that point, you can start writing, writing, and that's the real – that's the real – the icing on the cake.
So I was – I finished all my James Bond movies.
I'm not listening to anything else because I'm just too high, really, off of these edibles, you know.
You get really high off the edibles.
It's like you're not high enough and then all of a sudden you're too high and then you're like – and then you're like, wait, you're not high enough and then you're too high.
It's like trying to – it's like trying to, you know, do Google Earth with a really bad mouse connection, you know.
Trying to write a book where each time you type it, you have to wait 10 seconds for the words to appear, you know.
But – and overall, it's more expensive or a bit.
You know, if I was just a little bit more richer, then I could have formed to be high all the time.
Anybody who makes a reasonable amount of money should definitely be only getting high off the edibles and –
Because I'm having an asthma attack right now and I haven't – I haven't had a reefer for shit for a week or whatever.
So it's like, you know, so – but they say that after seven years, your body reinvents itself, so –
Recreates every single one of the cells, so, you know.
I won't have any black lung left after seven years from the last time I had a reefer, so I'll be good.
But, um, so, yeah, and then I watched – did you, Bruce Lee?
I just did a classic movie kick, and so right now I'm doing – I think I was just about to start to kill Bill,
But my roommate bought 12 beers, and he says six of them are for me, so I was like, okay.
He's like, yeah, isn't it badass to drink beer now that you're not as high?
And I'm like, oh, right, yeah, so I'll just drink beer as I'm running out of reefer gummies.
Ooh, 4.20, maybe that's it.
Okay, later.
